tool.name: check_known_vulnerabilities
tool.description:
Check the knowledge store for previously discovered vulnerabilities on a target.
Use this at the START of a scan to see what was already found in past runs. This helps avoid redundant testing and focuses your effort on new attack vectors.
Returns a list of known vulnerabilities with their severity, class, and endpoint.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The target URL, hostname, or IP to check (e.g., "http://example.com" or "10.0.0.1")

tool.name: enrich_vulnerability
tool.description:
Enrich a vulnerability finding with MITRE ATT&CK mappings (CWE, CAPEC) and compliance framework references (OWASP Top 10, PCI DSS, NIST).
Call this BEFORE create_vulnerability_report to get enrichment data you can include in the report description: - CWE IDs and names (Common Weakness Enumeration) - CAPEC IDs (Common Attack Pattern Enumeration) - OWASP Top 10 category - Compliance frameworks affected
This makes reports more professional and useful for compliance teams.

parameter.name: title
parameter.description: The vulnerability title (e.g., "SQL Injection in Login Endpoint")

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: Brief description of the vulnerability and how it was found

parameter.name: severity
parameter.description: Severity level: critical, high, medium, low, or info. Default: medium

tool.name: verify_vulnerability
tool.description:
Auto-verify a vulnerability using the verification engine.
Runs exploit-verification strategies (time-based SQLi, error-based SQLi, boolean injection, DOM reflection, LFI, SSTI) against the target to confirm exploitability.
Call this AFTER discovering a potential vulnerability but BEFORE create_vulnerability_report to increase confidence.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: Target URL where the vulnerability was found

parameter.name: vulnerability_class
parameter.description: Type of vulnerability: sqli, xss, lfi, ssti, idor, auth_bypass, etc.

parameter.name: severity
parameter.description: Severity level: critical, high, medium, low, info (default: medium)

returns.description: Verification results with: - verified: boolean indicating if vulnerability was confirmed - confidence: verification confidence score - evidence: proof of exploitation
